metadata.summary: Lightning-fast extraction of basic image metadata

documentation:
# imsize

[![Build Status](https://travis-ci.com/toaarnio/imsize.svg?branch=master)](https://travis-ci.com/github/toaarnio/imsize)

Lightning-fast extraction of image dimensions & bit depth. Tested on Python 3.8+ and Ubuntu 22.04.

Supports PGM / PPM / PNM / PFM / PNG / BMP / JPG / TIFF / INSP / EXR / HDR / DNG / CR2 / NEF / RAW / NPY.

**Installing on Linux:**
```
pip install imsize
```

**Documentation:**
```
pydoc imsize
```

**Command-line usage:**
```
imsize --help
```

**Building & installing from source:**
```
make install
```

**Building & releasing to PyPI:**
```
make release
```

modules.name: imsize.pfmhdr
documentation:
A minimal header parser for PFM images, meant solely for querying the
dimensions and bit depth.

functions.name: imsize.pfmhdr.dims
documentation:
Returns the dimensions (width, height, and number of channels) of the given
PFM file. Also returns the nominal scale of the pixel values (typically 1.0).
P U B L I C   A P I

functions.name: imsize.pfmhdr.__parse_header
documentation: I N T E R N A L   F U N C T I O N S

modules.name: imsize.exrhdr
documentation:
A minimal header parser for OpenEXR images, meant solely for querying the
dimensions and bit depth.

functions.name: imsize.exrhdr.dims
documentation:
Returns the dimensions (width, height, number of channels, datatype, and bit depth)
of the given EXR file.
P U B L I C   A P I

functions.name: imsize.exrhdr._read_string_nul
documentation: I N T E R N A L   F U N C T I O N S

modules.name: imsize
documentation:
Extracts image dimensions, bit depth, and other basic metadata.
Runs very fast due to reading and parsing only the file header,
with the exception of proprietary camera RAW files.

Example:
  info = imsize.read("myfile.jpg")
  factor = info.nbytes / info.filesize
  print(f"{info.filespec}: compression factor = {factor.1f}")

https://github.com/toaarnio/imsize

modules.name: imsize.pnmhdr
documentation:
A minimal header parser for PNM/PGM/PPM images, meant solely for querying the
dimensions and bit depth.

functions.name: imsize.pnmhdr.dims
documentation:
Returns the dimensions (width, height, and number of channels) of the given
PNM/PGM/PPM file. Also returns the maximum representable value of a pixel
(typically 255, 1023, 4095, or 65535).
P U B L I C   A P I

functions.name: imsize.pnmhdr.__parse_header
documentation: I N T E R N A L   F U N C T I O N S

modules.name: imsize.consoleapp
documentation:
Calculates the combined in-memory size of the given images by parsing
their header information. Runs very fast because only the header part
of a file is read from disk (except for headerless Camera RAW and Nikon
NEF).
!/usr/bin/python3 -B

functions.name: imsize.consoleapp.main
documentation: Entry point for the 'imsize' command-line application.

functions.name: imsize.consoleapp.find_files
documentation: Collects all files with known filetypes from the given list of directories.

functions.name: imsize.consoleapp.scan_sizes
documentation: Displays the dimensions of the given list of images.

functions.name: imsize.argv.filenames
documentation:
Examples:
  filenames, basenames = argv.filenames(sys.argv[1:], numRequired=2)
  filenames, basenames = argv.filenames(sys.argv[1:], [".ppm", ".png"], sort=True)
  filenames, basenames = argv.filenames(sys.argv[1:], [".jpg"], allowAllCaps=True)
pylint: disable=invalid-name
P U B L I C   A P I

functions.name: imsize.argv.exists
documentation:
Example:
  showHelp = argv.exists("--help")

functions.name: imsize.argv.intval
documentation:
Example:
  numtiles = argv.intval("--split", 1, [1, 2, 3, 4])

functions.name: imsize.argv.floatval
documentation:
Example:
  factor = argv.floatval("--factor", 1.0, condition='1.0 <= v <= 3.0')

functions.name: imsize.argv.stringval
documentation:
Examples:
  bayer = argv.stringval("--bayer", default="AUTO", accepted=["AUTO", "GBRG", "RGGB"])
  sources = argv.stringval("--source", default="all", accepted=["foo", "bar", "baz", "all"], repeats=True)

functions.name: imsize.argv.intpair
documentation:
Examples:
  width, height = argv.intpair("--size", default=(1920, 1080))
  limits = argv.intpair("--limits", default=(0, 1), repeats=True)

functions.name: imsize.argv.floatpair
documentation:
Example:
  factor1, factor2 = argv.floatpair("--factors", default=(1.0, 1.0))

functions.name: imsize.argv.floatstring
documentation:
Examples:
  blacklevel = argv.floatstring("--whitelevel", "AUTO", accepted=["AUTO"])
  whitelevel = argv.floatstring("--blacklevel", 1023.0, accepted=["AUTO"])

functions.name: imsize.argv.exitIfAnyUnparsedOptions
documentation:
Example:
  factor = argv.floatval("--factor", default=1.0)
  showHelp = argv.exists("--help")
  argv.exitIfAnyUnparsedOptions()

functions.name: imsize.argv._enforce
documentation: If 'expression' is False, prints out the given error message and exits.
I N T E R N A L   F U N C T I O N S

functions.name: imsize.argv._string
documentation: Parses arguments of the form '--argname string', returns the string.

functions.name: imsize.argv._isInt
documentation: Returns True if and only if the given string represents an integer.

functions.name: imsize.argv._isFloat
documentation: Returns True if and only if the given string represents a float.

functions.name: imsize.argv._isValid
documentation: Checks that 'arg' is in validArgs and satisfies the given condition.

classes.name: imsize.argv._Tests

methods.name: imsize.argv._Tests.test_exists
documentation: pylint: disable=missing-docstring
U N I T   T E S T S

modules.name: imsize.imsize
documentation:
Extracts image dimensions, bit depth, and other basic metadata.

Example:
  info = imsize.read("myfile.jpg")
  factor = info.nbytes / info.filesize
  print(f"{info.filespec}: compression factor = {factor.1f}")

https://github.com/toaarnio/imsize

functions.name: imsize.imsize.read
documentation:
Parses a lowest common denominator set of metadata from the given
image, i.e., the dimensions and bit depth. Does not read the entire
file but only what's necessary. If the file type is recognized and
parsing succeeds, returns an ImageInfo with all fields filled in.
Otherwise, an exception is raised or only the basic file attributes
(name, type, size) are filled in.

Example:
  info = imsize.read("myfile.jpg")
  factor = info.nbytes / info.filesize
  print(f"{info.filespec}: compression factor = {factor.1f}")

functions.name: imsize.imsize.silence_stdout
documentation: I N T E R N A L   F U N C T I O N S

classes.name: imsize.imsize.ImageInfo
documentation:
A container for image metadata, filled in and returned by read().

Attributes:
  filespec (str): The filespec given to read(), copied verbatim
  filetype (str): File type: png|pnm|pfm|bmp|jpeg|insp|tiff|exr|hdr|dng|cr2|nef|raw|npy|...
  filesize (int): Size of the file on disk in bytes
  multi_picture (bool): True if there is more than one image in the file
  num_images (int): Number of images contained in the file
  image_sizes (list): Size of each image in the file, in bytes
  image_offsets (list): Offset of each image in the file, in bytes
  header_size (int): Size of .raw file header in bytes
  isfloat (bool): True if the image is in floating-point format
  cfa_raw (bool): True if the image is in CFA (Bayer) raw format
  width (int): Width of the image in pixels (orientation ignored)
  height (int): Height of the image in pixels (orientation ignored)
  nchan (int): Number of color channels: 1, 2, 3 or 4
  bitdepth (int): Bits per sample: 1 to 32
  bytedepth (int): Bytes per sample: 1, 2 or 4
  maxval (int): Maximum representable sample value, e.g., 255
  nbytes (int): Size of the image in bytes, uncompressed
  orientation (int): Image orientation in EXIF format: 1 to 8, or 0 (unknown)
  rot90_ccw_steps (int): Number of rotations to bring the image upright: 0 to 3
  uncertain (bool): True if width/height/bitdepth are uncertain

classes.name: imsize.imsize.ImageFileError
documentation: A custom exception raised in all known error conditions.
